suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales
volcanic glass and its use in prehistoric surgery

formats.name: micro_flashback
formats.instruction: Share a vivid memory related to today's topic. Use sensory details. Limit to just 3 sentences, no more than 30 words.

formats.name: strange_comparison
formats.instruction: Compare the topic to something unrelated and bizarre. Explain how they're similar in a surprising way. Write in 2 short paragraphs, max 120 words.

formats.name: reverse_expert
formats.instruction: Write from the point of view of someone who has NO clue about the topic. List what confuses you, then ask for help. Use humor. 80-150 words.

formats.name: rapid_rant
formats.instruction: Rapid-fire vent about the topic. Use a list of 5-8 bullet points, each 1 sentence. Unfiltered emotion encouraged. Total words: 40-100.

formats.name: the_puzzle_piece
formats.instruction: Describe how the topic fits into a larger picture—what bigger story does it belong to? Write in 3 sections: 'The Piece', 'The Puzzle', 'My Hypothesis'. Up to 200 words.

formats.name: haiku_opinion
formats.instruction: State your opinion on the topic as a 3-line haiku—5, 7, 5 syllables. Then add a single sentence clarification.

formats.name: future_fiction
formats.instruction: Invent a scene from the future where the topic plays a central role. Write it as short fiction, in the present tense. 200-350 words.

formats.name: letter_to_past_self
formats.instruction: Write a brief letter to your younger self, warning them about the topic. Be warm, direct, and personal. 80-180 words.

formats.name: one_question
formats.instruction: Write a single, powerful question about the topic—no explanation, just the question. Must provoke deep thought.

title_styles: Craft a poetic, mystery-inspired title. For example: 'What the Shadows Reveal About X', 'Beneath the Surface of Y'.
Write a short, punchy challenge-style title. Examples: 'Prove Me Wrong About X', 'Dare to Rethink Y'.
Use a playful, pun or wordplay in the title. Examples: 'X Marks the Spot', 'Why Y is the Real MVP'.
Frame the title as the beginning of a story. Examples: 'Once upon a time, X', 'The day Y changed my mind'.
Pose a big question as the title. Examples: 'What if X never existed?', 'Could Y be the answer?'
structure_variants: Begin with an analogy, then unravel it with real facts.
Alternate between two perspectives throughout: supporter vs skeptic.
Include a single, vivid statistic or data point in the middle of the post.
Start with a metaphor, conclude with a literal takeaway.
Interject a brief imaginary scenario—'Picture this...'—to illustrate the main point.
topic_angles: Hidden social habits people rarely notice
Tech advances that shaped culture, not just innovation
Old myths still believed by modern people
Places where nature and technology unexpectedly blend
